text: Node.js powers scalable backend systems with its event-driven, non-blocking I/O model.
React builds dynamic user interfaces using a component-based architecture and virtual DOM.
RAG combines retrieval with LLM intelligence to provide accurate, context-aware responses.
Express.js is a minimal and flexible Node.js web application framework that provides robust features for web and mobile applications.
MongoDB is a NoSQL database that stores data in flexible, JSON-like documents, making it ideal for modern applications.
PostgreSQL is a powerful, open-source relational database system known for its reliability and feature completeness.
RESTful APIs use HTTP methods like GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE to perform CRUD operations on resources.
GraphQL is a query language for APIs that allows clients to request exactly the data they need, nothing more.
Docker containers provide consistent environments for development, testing, and deployment by packaging applications with their dependencies.
Kubernetes orchestrates containerized applications at scale, providing automated deployment, scaling, and management.
JWT (JSON Web Tokens) enable secure authentication between client and server without storing session state.
OAuth 2.0 is an authorization framework that enables third-party applications to obtain limited access to user accounts.
Redis is an in-memory data structure store used as a database, cache, and message broker for high-performance applications.
WebSocket enables real-time, bidirectional communication between web clients and servers over a single TCP connection.
TypeScript adds static typing to JavaScript, improving code quality and developer productivity with better IDE support.
Webpack bundles JavaScript modules and assets, optimizing them for production deployment with code splitting and tree shaking.
Microservices architecture breaks applications into small, independent services that communicate through well-defined APIs.
Serverless computing allows developers to run code without managing servers, paying only for actual compute time used.
CI/CD pipelines automate the build, test, and deployment process, enabling faster and more reliable software delivery.
Machine learning models can be deployed as REST APIs, enabling integration with web applications and mobile apps.
Vector databases store high-dimensional embeddings for efficient similarity search in AI applications.
Embeddings convert text, images, or other data into numerical vectors that capture semantic meaning.
Cosine similarity measures the similarity between two vectors by calculating the cosine of the angle between them.
Rate limiting protects APIs from abuse by restricting the number of requests a client can make in a time period.
CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) enables secure cross-origin requests between web browsers and servers.
Environment variables store configuration settings separately from code, enabling deployment across different environments.
Logging and monitoring are essential for debugging production issues and maintaining application health.
Unit testing validates individual components in isolation, ensuring code quality and preventing regressions.
Integration testing verifies that different components work together correctly as a complete system.
Responsive design ensures web applications work seamlessly across desktop, tablet, and mobile devices.
